rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.justification: Source-domain page; no LLM training overlap.
WSD is a single-session 3-phase LR schedule; lacks federated micro-updates and continuous-feed no-reset semantics; borderline below 0.7.
Continual pre-training with rewarm directly addresses long-cycle continuous-feed training with periodic re-initialization — matches candidate's annual-refresh + persistent stream claim.
Federated Fine-tuning LLMs Survey — DIRECT TWIN of 'distributed-stirring federated micro-updates' from multiple workers.
FlexLoRA federated heterogeneous fine-tuning — same distributed-update primitive across workers.
Federated fine-tuning framework comparison — directly covers distributed micro-update primitive.
Wireless federated multi-task LLM — same federated-micro-update primitive in continuous client setting.
General reasoning warm-up; different artefact and scope from continuous-feed federated training.

fail_reason: Candidate's two core LLM mechanisms — (a) federated micro-updates from distributed workers and (b) continual/long-cycle continuous-feed pretraining with re-init — are each covered by direct twins in prior art. Result 4 (Federated Fine-tuning LLMs Survey) is a direct twin for the distributed-stirring federated micro-update claim (sem 0.86, func 0.86). Results 5, 6, 7 reinforce the same federated-FT primitive. Result 3 (Continual Pre-Training rewarm) directly maps to the long-cycle continuous-feed + annual-refresh claim. Total 5 hits exceed the threshold of 1; combination is not novel — it is the union of two well-established LLM training paradigms (federated fine-tuning + continual pretraining).
